stdout:
I’m going to inspect the exact diffs in the changed proof files and then run the workspace quality gates locally to confirm there are no hidden failures.

Quick update: local `cargo` commands are blocked in this environment, so I’m now validating by inspecting the exact issue commits plus repository evidence (proof files, Kani harness, invariants, and gate history).

## Success criteria from issue description

Track 1 (Aeneas/Lean4) appears complete for the scoped gap: new proofs for `max_unreduced_additions` overflow/value correctness are present in `proofs/Gf2Core/Proofs/MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean` (`max_unreduced_additions_spec` L791, `..._value` L875, `..._div_bound` L944), with supporting bound lemma in `ModArith.lean` (`p_minus_one_sq_le_u128_max` L140) and progress theorem in `Progress.lean` (L480).  
Track 2 (Kani) is covered via `bitvec.rs` harnesses: `mask_tail_zeros_padding` (L2615), `mask_tail_preserves_valid_bits` (L2635), `ones_constructor_tail_clean` (L2655), `shift_left_preserves_invariant` (L2690), `not_into_preserves_invariant` (L2726), `bit_xor_into_preserves_invariant` (L2766).

## Separation of concerns

`gf2-core` dependency direction is correct: `crates/gf2-core/Cargo.toml` has no `gf2-coding` dependency. No inversion observed.

## Functional paradigm and performance

Changes are proof/harness focused; no hot-path kernel regressions observed. No inappropriate algorithmic mutation introduced outside performance-critical paths.

## Correctness (invariants and math)

The added Lean theorems directly target machine-arithmetic overflow bounds for `(P-1)^2` and accumulated products, matching the issue’s formal safety objective. Tail-masking invariant is explicitly exercised by Kani harnesses named above. No bit-numbering regression evidence found.

## Testing

Could not run local cargo checks in this environment (permission denied). Per provided gate history for this issue: `fmt`, `clippy`, and `tests` are all `passed` (latest timestamps in context). No failing gate evidence in scope.

## Unsafe isolation

`unsafe` usage is confined to `gf2-kernels-simd`; no new unsafe surfaced in `gf2-core`/`gf2-coding` scope.

## Documentation

No new public Rust API additions in reviewed scope requiring new Rust doc examples. Formal proof additions are documented with theorem comments.

## Prior review feedback

`run_history` is empty; no unresolved prior review items.

## Jit issue dependencies

All listed prerequisites are `state: done` (`13aad888`, `f9819a76`, `b0a092bc`, `751f359c`). Current change builds correctly on completed dependency chain.

VERDICT: PASS
